content.problemStatement:
Given an integer array AAA of length NNN, and an **odd** integer MMM, let f(A)f(A)f(A) denote the number of ordered pairs (i,j)(i, j)(i,j) such that 1‚Č§i<j‚Č§N1 \le i < j \le N1‚Č§i<j‚Č§N and Ai+Aj=MA_i + A_j = MAi‚Äč+Aj‚Äč=M.

You are also given a parameter KKK, and want to make f(A)‚Č•Kf(A) \ge Kf(A)‚Č•K. Find the minimum number of array replacements you need to perform to make f(A)‚Č•Kf(A) \ge Kf(A)‚Č•K.

Formally, in one replacement, you choose an index XXX, an integer YYY and change AXA_XAX‚Äč to YYY.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains NNN, MMM and KKK - the number of elements in the array, and the parameters MMM and KKK
The second line contains NNN integers - A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äč.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the minimum number of replacements needed. If it is impossible to make f(A)‚Č•Kf(A) \ge Kf(A)‚Č•K no matter what, output ‚ąí1-1‚ąí1 instead.
constraints: The sum of N3N^3N3 over all test cases does not exceed 10810^8108.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: Set A2=4A_2 = 4A2‚Äč=4, obtaining [1,4,1,4][1, 4, 1, 4][1,4,1,4] which has 444 pairs of elements that sum up to M=5M = 5M=5.
Test case 222: There are already two pairs of elements summing up to M=5M = 5M=5, and since K=2K = 2K=2 no moves are needed.
Test case 444: It's impossible to have ‚Č•2\geq 2‚Č•2 pairs of elements sum up to M=3M = 3M=3 no matter what is done.
